4) School Level Committee
The School Management Committee / School Management and Development Committee at the school level is the nodal body for the programme.
The school will function as a level committee.
The school level is responsible for making and implementing all policy decisions at the school level.
There will be a committee.
3. To act in accordance with all the instructions given at the state, district and block levels.
shall be binding on the Committee.
The functions of the State Level, District Level, Group Level and School Level Committees shall be as follows.
a) State Literacy Mission Authority (SLMA)
The Governing Council: -
1) To prepare the State Plan of the New India Literacy Programme Scheme.
2) To campaign for the implementation of the New India Literacy Programme scheme.
3) The Central Government and the States for implementation of the New India Literacy Programme Scheme.
Allocation and accounting of funds received from the government to the implementing agencies.
4) Monitoring of district planning.
5) To ensure that the assessment is well done and the information is open to the public.
6) Strategic planning, implementation and evaluation of the New India Literacy Programme, etc.
to make a decision.
7) Administrative finances to achieve physical and financial objectives under the Navbharat Literacy Programme.
to make decisions about the matter.
8) To decide on the proposals recommended by the Executive Committee.

para_trans: Government Decision No: NABASA-1626 / P.No.84 / SD2
The Executive Committee: -
1) The policy decision taken by the Regulatory Council of the State Literacy Mission Authority.
to implement.
2) To take action on matters empowered by the Regulatory Council.
3) To make recommendations to the Regulatory Council for making policy decisions.
4) Taking the help of the media to promote the New India Literacy Programme at the state level.
5) Appointment of contractual staff on contract basis for State / District Office.
6) Centre for State Literacy Centres (SCLs) set up in the State Council of Education, Research and Training.
To issue directions in accordance with the guidelines received from the Government from time to time.
(b) The District Level Committee
The Governing Council:
1) Planning of the New India Literacy Programme at the district level as per the State Plan.
2) To carry out campaigns at the district level for the implementation of the New India Literacy Programme scheme.
3) Funds received from the State Government for implementation of the New India Literacy Programme Scheme.
Allocation to and accounting for implementing agencies.
4) Monitoring the planning of the taluks.
(vi) Necessary to achieve the goals and objectives set by the State Literacy Mission Authority.
to make policy decisions.
6) To ensure that the assessment is well done and the information is open to the public.
7) Strategic planning, implementation and evaluation of the New India Literacy Programme, etc.
to make a decision.
8) Administrative finances to achieve physical and financial objectives under the Navbharat Literacy Programme.
to make decisions about the matter.
9) To decide on the proposals recommended by the District Level Executive Committee.
The Executive Committee:
1) The policy decision taken by the Regulatory Council of the State Literacy Mission Authority.
implementation at the district level.
2) To take action on matters empowered by the District Level Regulatory Council.
3) To make recommendations to the District Regulatory Council for taking policy decisions.

a) Taluk Level Committee
The Governing Council:
1) New India Literacy Programme Scheme at Taluka level as per State / District Plan
to make planning.
2) Campaign at Taluka level for implementation of New India Literacy Programme Scheme.
3) State Governments / Districts for implementation of New India Literacy Programme Scheme.
Allocating and accounting for the funds received to the implementing agencies.
4) Monitoring of activities at the village level.

to make policy decisions.
6) To ensure that the assessment is well done and the information is open to the public.
7) Strategic planning, implementation and evaluation of the New India Literacy Programme, etc.
to make a decision.
8) At the Taluka level to achieve the physical and financial objectives under the Navbharat Literacy Programme.
Deciding on administrative financial matters.
9) To decide on the proposals recommended by the Taluk Level Executive Committee.
The Executive Committee: -
1) Taluk of the policy decision taken by the Regulatory Council of the State Literacy Mission Authority
implementation at the level.
2) To take action on matters empowered by the Taluk Level Regulatory Council.
3) To make recommendations to the Taluka Regulatory Council for taking policy decisions.
4) Taking the help of the media to promote the New India Literacy Programme at the Taluka level.
5) Appointment of contractual staff on contract basis for Taluk / Village Office.
